Web search: Mayo Clinic, Drugs.com, MedlinePlus confirm Amoxicillin is real FDA-approved antibiotic
Web search (Mayo Clinic, Drugs.com): Standard adult dosage is 250-500mg every 8 hours. 500mg is appropriate for oral amoxicillin.
Web search (Mayo Clinic, MedlinePlus): Confirms 'Amoxicillin (oral route)' is standard. PO = per os = oral administration.
Web search (Mayo Clinic, Drugs.com): Standard dosing is '500mg every 8 hours' which equals TID (three times daily)
Web search (Mayo Clinic, UCSF IDMP): Typical antibiotic courses are 7-28 days. 10 days is standard for infections.
Medical standard: Antibiotic durations measured in days
Mathematical verification: 3 times daily × 10 days = 30 tablets. Confirmed by seed_patients.sql drug table schema.

privileged_info_summary: Patient Agnes Miller is a synthetic female patient (DOB: 1987-06-22) registered in the OSCAR EMR system with demographic record. The task requires prescribing Amoxicillin 500mg orally three times daily (TID) for 10 days, resulting in 30 tablets total. All specified dosing parameters are verified as medically standard: 500mg every 8 hours is a standard adult dosage for amoxicillin, 10-day antibiotic courses are standard practice, and PO (oral) is the standard route. The OSCAR EMR system runs on localhost:8080 with default demo credentials (oscardoc/oscar, PIN: 1117). All task metadata values are verified and consistent with medical standards and system configuration.
